title: lobelia acutangula
intro: Lobelia acutangula, also known as Indian tobacco or Khatmuli, is a flowering plant species native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, helping to relieve respiratory issues and muscle spasms. The plant is rich in alkaloids, including lobeline, which has been shown to have health benefits such as improving respiratory function, reducing inflammation, and even exhibiting neuroprotective effects. Lobelia acutangula is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional Indian medicine, Ayurveda, where it is valued for its ability to balance the body's energy and promote overall well-being.
